evidence_quotes.quote: Right now, the publishers have the right to terminate your game after you've purchased it, and after they do so, the game is in an unplayable state.

paragraphs.text: Since this talks about getting rid of unfair commercial practices, I would also like video games to be added into this. Right now, the publishers have the right to terminate your game after you've purchased it, and after they do so, the game is in an unplayable state. In simpler terms, the player cannot use the goods that they've purchased. Publishers often put this into the end-user licence agreement, that they can terminate your game at any time for any/no reason at all, but this seems malicious and may actually violate some consumer laws in the EU. So me, and many other people that have signed the "Stop Killing Games" petition which has reached over 1,4 million signatures would like you to consider adding videogames to the discussion.
